question: Consider the discrete stacked triangular antiferromagnet (STA) in which spins reside on a three-dimensional lattice of stacked triangular layers. Spins are grouped into non-overlapping 'supertriangles' whose three constituent unit-length Heisenberg spins are constrained to form a 120-degree coplanar configuration (local rigidity constraint). The exchange coupling between neighboring spins sets the energy scale J. Periodic boundary conditions are used and lattice linear sizes L = 12, 18, 24, 30, 36 are studied. What is the infinite-system critical temperature T_c(infinity) in units of J (i.e., k_B T_c / J), determined via histogram Monte Carlo and finite-size scaling? Output a JSON object {"kBTc_over_J": <value>}.

metadata.system: Heisenberg stacked triangular AFM, rigid-triangle constraint, L=12-36
metadata.source_excerpt: extensive histogram Monte Carlo simulations on periodic lattices with linear sizes L=12,18,24,30,36 yield a second-order phase transition with infinite-system critical temperature T_c(infinity)=1.431+/-0.001 (in units where J sets the energy scale).
metadata.difficulty_note: Heisenberg stacked triangular antiferromagnet with local 120-degree rigidity, histogram MC+FSS
